Lumify
Table of Contents

Class EventsController

Namespace
lumify.api.Controllers
Assembly
lumify.api.dll

Manages calendar events, both personal (no workspace) and workspace-shared, including creation, editing, deletion and various queries (counts, lists, linked workspace). All endpoints require an authenticated user. Changes to workspace events are pushed live to the workspace group over the EventHub.

[ApiController]
[Route("events/[action]")]
[Authorize]
public class EventsController : ControllerBase
Inheritance
object
ControllerBase
EventsController

Remarks

Permission rule throughout: a personal event may only be modified by its owner, while a workspace event may be modified by any active member of that workspace (see CanModify). For privacy, the creator is surfaced only as display name → username → ID, never the real first/last name.

Constructors

EventsController(ILogger<EventsController>, LumifyDbContext, IHubContext<EventHub>)

Creates the controller with its injected logger, database context and the event hub used for live notifications.

public EventsController(ILogger<EventsController> logger, LumifyDbContext db, IHubContext<EventHub> eventHub)

Parameters

logger ILogger<EventsController>
db LumifyDbContext
eventHub IHubContext<EventHub>

Methods

AddEvent(AddEventRequest, CancellationToken)

Creates a new calendar event, optionally inside a workspace.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("addEvent")]
public Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>> AddEvent(AddEventRequest request, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

request AddEventRequest

Event data (name, start/end time required; optional description, all-day, workspace).

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>>

200 with the created event; 400 on missing/invalid fields; 403 if the user is not a member of the target workspace.

Remarks

Start and end times must be valid dates and end must not be before start. If a workspace is given, the user must be a member of it. On success in a workspace, an EventCreated event is broadcast to the workspace group.

DeleteEvent(string, CancellationToken)

Soft-deletes a calendar event.

[HttpDelete]
[ActionName("deleteEvent")]
public Task<ActionResult> DeleteEvent(string eventID, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

eventID string

The event to delete.

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult>

200 with success = true and the event ID; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user may not delete it; 404 if the event does not exist.

Remarks

The record is kept and marked with DeletedAt. On a workspace event, an EventDeleted event is broadcast to the workspace group.

GetAllEventsOfUser(CancellationToken)

Returns all personal events (no workspace) of the current user, ordered by start date.

[HttpGet]
[ActionName("getAllEventsOfUser")]
public Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>> GetAllEventsOfUser(CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>>

200 with the list of events.

GetAllEventsOfWorkspace(string, CancellationToken)

Returns all events of a workspace, ordered by start date. The user must be a member.

[HttpGet]
[ActionName("getAllEventsOfWorkspace")]
public Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>> GetAllEventsOfWorkspace(string workspaceID, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

workspaceID string

The workspace whose events are requested.

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult<List<EventResponse>>>

200 with the list of events; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user is not a member; 404 if the workspace does not exist.

Remarks

Events whose creator was soft-deleted are kept (content belongs to the workspace) and surface the creator as "Gelöschter Benutzer".

GetEventCountOfUser(CancellationToken)

Returns the number of personal events (no workspace) owned by the current user.

[HttpGet]
[ActionName("getEventCountOfUser")]
public Task<ActionResult<int>> GetEventCountOfUser(CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult<int>>

200 with the event count.

GetEventCountOfWorkspace(string, CancellationToken)

Returns the number of events in a workspace. The user must be a member.

[HttpGet]
[ActionName("getEventCountOfWorkspace")]
public Task<ActionResult<int>> GetEventCountOfWorkspace(string workspaceID, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

workspaceID string

The workspace to count events for.

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult<int>>

200 with the event count; 400 if the ID is missing; 403 if the user is not a member; 404 if the workspace does not exist.

GetSpaceInfosOfEvent(string, CancellationToken)

Returns the workspace that the given event belongs to, if any.

[HttpGet]
[ActionName("getSpaceInfosOfEvent")]
public Task<ActionResult<WorkspaceResponse>> GetSpaceInfosOfEvent(string eventID, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

eventID string

The event whose workspace is requested.

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult<WorkspaceResponse>>

200 with the workspace; 400 if the ID is missing; 404 if the event has no linked workspace or it was deleted.

SaveEvent(SaveEventRequest, CancellationToken)

Updates an event's name, description, all-day flag and/or start/end times. Only the provided fields are changed, and the database is only written if something changed.

[HttpPatch]
[ActionName("saveEvent")]
public Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>> SaveEvent(SaveEventRequest request, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

request SaveEventRequest

The event ID plus the fields to change.

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<ActionResult<EventResponse>>

200 with the updated event; 400 on invalid input; 403 if the user may not modify it; 404 if the event does not exist.

Remarks

On a successful change in a workspace, an EventUpdated event is broadcast to the workspace group.
